title: 301. Which Indian monument is known as the 'Symbol of Love' and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
hint: It was built by a Mughal emperor for his wife.
summary: The Taj Mahal in Agra is one of the most famous UNESCO World Heritage Sites in the world.

title: 302. In which city is the Taj Mahal located?
hint: The city lies on the banks of the Yamuna River.
summary: The Taj Mahal is located in Agra, Uttar Pradesh.

title: 303. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site in Delhi is the tallest brick minaret in India?
hint: It was started by Qutb-ud-din Aibak.
summary: Qutub Minar is one of Delhi's most famous historical monuments.

title: 304. Which UNESCO site in Delhi served as the residence of Mughal emperors?
hint: It is built from red sandstone.
summary: The Red Fort was the principal residence of Mughal emperors.

title: 305. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site is known as the precursor to the Taj Mahal?
hint: It is the tomb of a Mughal emperor in Delhi.
summary: Humayun's Tomb introduced Persian architectural elements later perfected in the Taj Mahal.

title: 306. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site is famous for rock-cut Buddhist caves in Maharashtra?
hint: The caves contain paintings depicting Jataka tales.
summary: Ajanta Caves are renowned for ancient Buddhist art and paintings.

title: 307. Which UNESCO site contains Hindu, Buddhist and Jain cave monuments at one location?
hint: It is located near Aurangabad.
summary: Ellora Caves showcase religious harmony through diverse cave temples.

title: 308. The famous Kailasa Temple is located in which UNESCO World Heritage Site?
hint: It is a monolithic rock-cut temple.
summary: The Kailasa Temple is the most famous monument at Ellora Caves.

title: 309. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site near Mumbai is dedicated primarily to Lord Shiva?
hint: Located on an island in Mumbai Harbour.
summary: Elephanta Caves are famous for the giant Trimurti sculpture of Shiva.

title: 310. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site in Karnataka was the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire?
hint: It is located on the banks of the Tungabhadra River.
summary: Hampi was the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire.

title: 311. Which UNESCO site in Karnataka is known as a 'laboratory of temple architecture'?
hint: It contains both Nagara and Dravida styles.
summary: Pattadakal showcases the evolution of temple architecture.

title: 312. The Group of Monuments at Mahabalipuram is located in which state?
hint: Its capital is Chennai.
summary: Mahabalipuram is famous for Pallava-era rock-cut architecture.

title: 313. Which dynasty built most monuments at Mahabalipuram?
hint: Their capital was Kanchipuram.
summary: The Pallavas created the famous monuments at Mahabalipuram.

title: 314. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site is associated with the Chola dynasty?
hint: It includes Brihadeeswarar Temple.
summary: The Great Living Chola Temples are among India's finest examples of Dravidian architecture.

title: 315. Brihadeeswarar Temple is located in which city?
hint: The city was a major Chola capital.
summary: Brihadeeswarar Temple is located in Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu.

title: 316. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site in Odisha is shaped like a giant chariot?
hint: Dedicated to the Sun God.
summary: Konark Sun Temple is one of India's most iconic monuments.

title: 317. Konark Sun Temple was built by which dynasty?
hint: The dynasty ruled large parts of Odisha.
summary: The Eastern Ganga Dynasty built the Konark Sun Temple.

title: 318. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site is famous for Buddhist stupas in Madhya Pradesh?
hint: Emperor Ashoka's wife is believed to have been connected to this place.
summary: The Buddhist Monuments at Sanchi are among India's oldest stone structures.

title: 319. Which emperor is most closely associated with the development of Sanchi?
hint: He embraced Buddhism after the Kalinga War.
summary: Emperor Ashoka commissioned the original stupa at Sanchi.

title: 320. Nalanda Mahavihara, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is located in which state?
hint: The state capital is Patna.
summary: Nalanda was one of the world's earliest residential universities.

title: 321. Which Chinese traveler studied at Nalanda University?
hint: He visited India during the reign of Harsha.
summary: Xuanzang spent several years studying at Nalanda.

title: 322. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site marks the place where Buddha attained enlightenment?
hint: The Mahabodhi Temple is located here.
summary: Bodh Gaya is one of the holiest Buddhist pilgrimage sites.

title: 323. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site in Rajasthan is famous for astronomical instruments?
hint: Built by Sawai Jai Singh II.
summary: Jantar Mantar in Jaipur is an astronomical observatory.

title: 324. Jaipur became a UNESCO World Heritage City in which year?
hint: The year was after 2018 and before 2020.
summary: Jaipur was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage City in 2019.

title: 325. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site is known as the 'Pink City'?
hint: It is the capital of Rajasthan.
summary: Jaipur is popularly known as the Pink City and is a UNESCO World Heritage City.

title: 326. Kaziranga National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, is located in which state?
hint: The Brahmaputra River flows through this state.
summary: Kaziranga is famous for the world's largest population of one-horned rhinoceroses.

title: 327. Kaziranga National Park is best known for conserving which animal?
hint: It has a single horn.
summary: Kaziranga contains the largest population of Indian one-horned rhinoceroses.

title: 328. Manas Wildlife Sanctuary is situated along the border of India and which country?
hint: The capital of this country is Thimphu.
summary: Manas forms a transboundary conservation landscape with Bhutan.

title: 329. Manas Wildlife Sanctuary is located in which Indian state?
hint: It is famous for tea gardens and the Brahmaputra Valley.
summary: Manas Wildlife Sanctuary is a UNESCO Natural World Heritage Site in Assam.

title: 330. Keoladeo National Park is located in which Rajasthan city?
hint: The city was formerly known as Bharatpur.
summary: Keoladeo is one of the world's most important bird habitats.

title: 331. Keoladeo National Park is especially famous for which category of animals?
hint: Many migratory species visit annually.
summary: Keoladeo is internationally recognized as a bird sanctuary.

title: 332. Valley of Flowers National Park is located in which state?
hint: The state is known as Devbhoomi.
summary: Valley of Flowers is renowned for its alpine meadows and endemic flora.

title: 333. Nanda Devi National Park and Valley of Flowers together form which UNESCO property?
hint: The name includes a famous Himalayan peak.
summary: The combined UNESCO site is called Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks.

options.title: Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks

title: 334. Sundarbans National Park is famous for which animal?
hint: It is the national animal of India.
summary: The Sundarbans contain one of the largest populations of Royal Bengal Tigers.

title: 335. Sundarbans is the world's largest ecosystem of which type?
hint: Trees here can tolerate saline water.
summary: The Sundarbans contain the world's largest mangrove forest.

title: 336. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site extends across six Indian states?
hint: It is one of the world's biodiversity hotspots.
summary: The Western Ghats stretch from Gujarat to Kerala and Tamil Nadu.

title: 337. Great Himalayan National Park is located in which state?
hint: Shimla is the capital of this state.
summary: Great Himalayan National Park became a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2014.

title: 338. Khangchendzonga National Park is located in which state?
hint: It is home to India's highest mountain peak.
summary: Khangchendzonga National Park is India's first Mixed UNESCO World Heritage Site.

title: 339. Khangchendzonga National Park is India's first UNESCO site in which category?
hint: It combines natural and cultural significance.
summary: Khangchendzonga became India's first Mixed World Heritage Site.

title: 340. The Hill Forts of Rajasthan UNESCO property includes how many forts?
hint: More than five but less than ten.
summary: The Hill Forts of Rajasthan consist of six majestic forts.

title: 341. Which of the following is NOT part of the Hill Forts of Rajasthan UNESCO property?
hint: This fort is located in Agra.
summary: Agra Fort is a UNESCO site but not part of the Hill Forts of Rajasthan.

title: 342. Churches and Convents of Goa primarily reflect the influence of which European power?
hint: Vasco da Gama reached India under this country's flag.
summary: The monuments of Goa reflect Portuguese colonial architecture.

title: 343. Which saint's remains are preserved in the Basilica of Bom Jesus?
hint: A famous Jesuit missionary.
summary: The Basilica of Bom Jesus houses the mortal remains of St. Francis Xavier.

title: 344. Victorian Gothic and Art Deco Ensembles is a UNESCO site located in which city?
hint: India's financial capital.
summary: The ensemble showcases architectural evolution in Mumbai.

title: 345. Which city became India's first UNESCO World Heritage City?
hint: It is the largest city in Gujarat.
summary: Ahmedabad became India's first UNESCO World Heritage City in 2017.

title: 346. Dholavira is associated with which ancient civilization?
hint: It flourished around 2500 BCE.
summary: Dholavira is one of the most remarkable cities of the Indus Valley Civilization.

title: 347. Dholavira is located in which Indian state?
hint: The Rann of Kutch is located here.
summary: Dholavira is situated on Khadir Bet in Gujarat.

title: 348. Ramappa Temple is located in which state?
hint: Its capital is Hyderabad.
summary: Ramappa Temple became a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2021.

title: 349. Ramappa Temple was built during the rule of which dynasty?
hint: Their capital was Warangal.
summary: Ramappa Temple is a masterpiece of Kakatiya architecture.

title: 350. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site was inscribed in 2024 and is associated with the Ahom dynasty?
hint: These are royal burial mounds in Assam.
summary: The Moidams of Charaideo became India's latest UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2024.
